summary: Matched the 488-byte item source file `src/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.c`, replacing the old assembly file `asm/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.s` in the build. The matched code implements item spawn/setup, callbacks, lifetime decrement, physics update, collision/death check, and a wrapper to `it_8026B894`. Supporting header/prototype cleanups were made so the item code could typecheck and match, including changing `Item_80268B18` to return `Item_GObj*` and adding a prototype for `it_8026DFB0`. The PR report says the file and all six functions reached 100.00% with score 0.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.s`, which contained 167 lines of hand-written/disassembled assembly for the item file.

Added `src/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.c` with C implementations for `it_802ADA1C`, `it_802ADAF0`, `it_802ADB48`, `it_802ADB80`, `it_802ADBB0`, and `it_802ADBE4`, plus the `ItemStateTable it_803F6C88` data object.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so the build uses `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.c.o` instead of `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.s.o`.

`it_802ADA1C` constructs a `SpawnItem`, sets `kind = It_Kind_Unk1`, copies position/velocity inputs, forces `prev_pos.z = 0`, sets parent object fields to `NULL`, enables `spawnitem.x44_flag.bits.b0`, calls `Item_80268B18`, then initializes the spawned item from special attributes via `it_80275158` and `it_802ADAF0`.

`it_802ADAF0` calls `it_8026B3A8`, starts animation update with `Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)`, and clears `item->xDCE_flag.bits.b7`; it retains a `MUST_MATCH` unused stack byte array for matching.
`it_802ADB48` decrements `GET_ITEM(gobj)->xD44_lifeTimer` by 1 and returns true once the timer is less than or equal to zero.
`it_802ADB80` fetches `ItemAttr` from `GET_ITEM(gobj)->xCC_item_attr` and forwards fall speed values to `it_80272860`.
`it_802ADBB0` returns the boolean result of `it_8026DFB0()` using a ternary form, matching the original branch shape.
Changed `Item_80268B18` in `src/melee/it/item.h` from returning `HSD_GObj*` to returning `Item_GObj*`, allowing the new item code to use item-specific object typing directly.
Added `/* 26DFB0 */ bool it_8026DFB0(void);` to `src/melee/it/it_266F.h` for the new collision/death check callback.
Updated `src/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.h` to expose `it_802ADA1C`, `it_802ADBE4`, and `it_803F6C88`, and added `<placeholder.h>` to satisfy currently unresolved type/header needs.

Made several nearby formatting or include-order adjustments in unrelated files such as `itmetamon.c`, `itmato.c`, `OSInit.c`, `OSTime.h`, `CARDBios.c`, `ftdemo.c`, `ftmaterial.c`, and `cobj.c`; these appear to be incidental style or matching-sensitive changes rather than functional changes.
smart_moves: Kept the item state callbacks static in the C file while exporting only the externally needed entry points and data table through `it_2ADA.h`.

Used typed item accessors and structs (`Item_GObj`, `Item`, `ItemAttr`, `SpawnItem`, `ItemStateTable`) instead of preserving raw offset logic from the assembly, improving future readability without losing the match.
Corrected `Item_80268B18` to return `Item_GObj*`, which better reflects how item spawn results are consumed by item code and reduces local casts or generic `HSD_GObj*` leakage.

Preserved a `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack allocation in `it_802ADAF0`, explicitly documenting it with `@todo Unused stack`; this isolates a matching artifact without forcing it into normal non-matching builds.

Expressed bitfield behavior directly through known fields (`spawnitem.x44_flag.bits.b0`, `item->xDCE_flag.bits.b7`) instead of manual byte/rlwimi operations, confirming useful struct/bitfield layout knowledge.
Used a one-entry `ItemStateTable` initializer matching the old `.data` object `it_803F6C88`, keeping callback order visible and reusable for future item matches.
decomp_lessons: When matching item spawn routines, reconstructing the stack `SpawnItem` layout is often the central task: field order, zeroed parent pointers, `x3C_damage`, `x40`, and `x44_flag.bits.b0` all affect codegen.
For assembly that loads item special attributes through `item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`, naming and typing that path can turn opaque offset chains into clean C while still matching.
Small boolean wrappers may need source forms that preserve branch/codegen shape, such as `return it_8026DFB0() ? true : false;` rather than assuming direct return will match.
A tiny file match can require broad header hygiene: missing prototypes and inaccurate return types outside the target file can block clean C or force worse local code.
Matching artifacts should be fenced with `MUST_MATCH` and documented when possible, so future cleanup can revisit them without obscuring intended logic.
Changing `obj_files.mk` from the asm object to the C object is the final integration step for an asm-to-C match and should accompany deletion of the old `.s` file.
naming_conventions: Unidentified item file and function names retained address-based names such as `it_2ADA`, `it_802ADA1C`, `it_802ADAF0`, `it_802ADB48`, `it_802ADB80`, `it_802ADBB0`, and `it_802ADBE4`.
Item globals retain address-based names with section/address comments, e.g. `/* 3F6C88 */ extern ItemStateTable it_803F6C88[];`.
Header prototypes use address comments such as `/* 2ADA1C */`, `/* 2ADBE4 */`, and `/* 26DFB0 */` to preserve mapping to original binary addresses.
Item object variables use local names like `gobj`, `item`, `ip`, and `attrs`; `GET_ITEM(gobj)` is used for conversion from item gobj to item data.

Struct fields follow existing decomp offset-prefixed naming, e.g. `xD44_lifeTimer`, `xDCE_flag.bits.b7`, `xCC_item_attr`, `xC4_article_data`, `x4_specialAttributes`, `x10_fall_speed`, and `x14_fall_speed_max`.
Function pointer spacing was normalized in `it_2725.h` from `void(*)(Item_GObj*)` to `void (*)(Item_GObj*)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly constants `it_804DCF28` and `it_804DCF2C` with C literals/expressions: zeroing `prev_pos.z` and subtracting `1` from `xD44_lifeTimer`.
Matched the assembly stack initialization of `SpawnItem` by assigning fields in a precise sequence in `it_802ADA1C`, including copy-by-value for `Vec3` inputs.
Used a scoped block around `Item_GObj* gobj = Item_80268B18(&spawnitem);` in `it_802ADA1C`, likely to influence local lifetime/register allocation for the match.
Added `#ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 _[4] = { 0 }; #endif` in `it_802ADAF0` to account for an unused stack slot visible in the original assembly.
Kept `it_802ADBB0` as an explicit ternary boolean conversion, mirroring the original compare-and-branch return pattern.

Several whitespace-only macro formatting changes, such as `((x) &0x7f)`, `((ticks) *8)`, and `((a) *0.01745329252F)`, may be clang-format or compiler-tokenization workarounds in this codebase; the dump does not provide review evidence explaining them.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The only explicit PR-body rationale was `Resolves #1057` plus the matching report showing 100.00% for the file and every listed function.
follow_up_queries: Look for issue #1057 to understand the original target or motivation for matching `it_2ADA`.
Search later PRs for removal or explanation of `<placeholder.h>` in `src/melee/it/items/it_2ADA.h`.
Search later PRs for renaming of `it_2ADA`, `It_Kind_Unk1`, or the functions `it_802ADA1C` through `it_802ADBE4` to semantic item names.
Search for other PRs that changed `Item_80268B18` return type or item spawn typing to confirm this convention became standard.
Search for `MUST_MATCH` unused stack patterns in item files to reuse the approach for similar small stack mismatches.
